skill.name: check-migration
skill.description: Checks Flyway migration files for version conflicts, naming rules, simple SQL quality signals, and conflicts with the main branch.
skill.summary: Validate Flyway migration files before merging database changes.
skill.author: Crearize

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found shell command examples, a weak-crypto alert, and a high-entropy alert in SKILL.md. The crypto and entropy alerts are false positives from descriptive Markdown and Japanese text, but the shell and git command instructions are real external-command usage and should be shown with a warning.

medium_findings.title: External Shell and Git Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs the agent to run shell commands such as ls, grep, git fetch, and git diff. These commands are legitimate for migration review, but they access the filesystem and contact the configured git remote.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly present in Markdown bash blocks and match the skill's stated purpose. Risk is moderate because commands are hardcoded examples, but git fetch performs network access.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged line 3 as a weak cryptographic algorithm, but that line is a Japanese description of migration checks. No cryptographic algorithm or crypto API usage is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains only frontmatter description text about Flyway migration validation. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA1, DES, or related weak crypto usage.

low_findings.title: High Entropy Alert Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged high entropy at line 1, but the file is readable Markdown with YAML frontmatter and Japanese content. No binary, encrypted, or obfuscated payload is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The complete file is human-readable Markdown and contains no encoded blocks or hidden payloads. The entropy score is likely influenced by non-English text.

dangerous_patterns.title: Command Examples in Skill Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The Markdown includes bash command examples that an agent may execute during migration checks. Commands should be reviewed before use in untrusted repositories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The evidence is direct because the command snippets are visible in fenced bash blocks. The commands appear operational rather than malicious.

content.value_statement: Database migration mistakes can break deployments or create merge conflicts late in review. This skill guides Claude, Codex, or Claude Code through Flyway file checks before changes are merged.
actual_capabilities: Lists Flyway migration files in the expected project directory.
Checks migration file names against the V[number]__description.sql pattern.
Identifies duplicate, missing, or conflicting migration version numbers.
Compares migration changes against origin/main using git commands.
Reviews simple SQL quality signals such as CREATE TABLE and IF NOT EXISTS usage.
Produces a concise completion report with migration count and check results.
limitations: Assumes migrations are stored under backend/src/main/resources/db/migration.
Performs simple SQL checks and does not replace a full SQL parser.
Requires repository access and git remote availability for main branch comparison.
Does not run Flyway itself or verify database runtime behavior.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge migration review
use_cases.description: Check new migration files before a pull request is merged into main.

use_cases.title: Release readiness check
use_cases.description: Confirm migration numbering, naming, and main branch conflicts before a release cut.

use_cases.title: Database change hygiene
use_cases.description: Apply a consistent checklist for SQL migration files across a development team.

prompt_templates.title: Check current migrations
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner check for a local branch
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the check-migration skill to review the Flyway migration files in this branch. Report naming, version, and conflict issues.

prompt_templates.title: Review a pull request
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the check-migration skill for this pull request. Compare migration files with origin/main and summarize any release risks.

prompt_templates.title: Audit migration numbering
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the check-migration skill to inspect migration version numbers. Identify duplicates, missing sequence numbers, and conflicting branch changes.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare release migration report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the check-migration skill to produce a release-ready migration report with file counts, latest version, branch conflicts, and SQL quality concerns.

output_examples.input: Review the migrations in my feature branch before merge.
output_examples.output: The report lists total migration files, latest version, naming status, duplicate version status, and main branch conflict status.

output_examples.input: Check whether this release branch has migration conflicts.
output_examples.output: The response highlights migration files changed on the branch and notes whether any version numbers conflict with origin/main.

output_examples.input: Validate a new create table migration.
output_examples.output: The response confirms the file naming pattern, version sequence, and whether simple SQL safeguards such as IF NOT EXISTS are present.

best_practices: Run the skill before merging branches that add Flyway migrations.
Review git commands before execution when working with untrusted remotes.
Use the output as a checklist, then run project tests or Flyway validation.
anti_patterns: Do not treat simple SQL checks as full database compatibility validation.
Do not run git fetch against remotes you do not trust.
Do not ignore migration conflicts because filenames appear valid.

faq.question: What migration tool does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Flyway migration files that use versioned SQL filenames.

faq.question: Does it execute database migrations?
faq.answer: No. It reviews files and reports issues. It does not run Flyway or change a database.

faq.question: Can it find branch conflicts?
faq.answer: Yes. It compares migration file changes with origin/main when git access is available.

faq.question: Does it validate all SQL syntax?
faq.answer: No. It performs simple checks and should be paired with project-level SQL validation.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill includes shell and git command examples. These are useful, but they should be reviewed before execution.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
